dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI got the clearest direct lift on the front page: ChatGPT was discussed as surfacing a useful mathematical idea in an Erdős problem, the Privacy Filter release drew constructive technical interest, and the SWE-bench post read more like benchmark housekeeping than a reputational setback [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won because it had the broadest and strongest direct coverage among the tracked providers. The most visible signal was the ChatGPT math story, where commenters treated the model as having contributed a genuinely useful angle even while noting expert cleanup was needed [E1]. OpenAI also benefited from a technical launch thread around its Privacy Filter model that was mostly constructive rather than hostile [E2]. Its SWE-bench post generated skepticism about benchmarks in general, but that discussion did not turn into a focused anti-OpenAI pile-on [E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT drew attention for helping surface a novel route on an Erdős problem, and OpenAI's Privacy Filter release got technically engaged discussion about an open local redaction model; the SWE-bench thread mostly added context about benchmark saturation and gaming rather than direct damage to OpenAI's reputation [E1][E2][E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Anthropic got a notable boost from a top comment congratulating it for saturating SWE-bench Verified, but that was partly tempered by a separate complaint that Anthropic changes have made agent context and thinking traces harder to inspect [E4][E5].

evidence.summary: The ChatGPT/Erdős story framed OpenAI's model as helping uncover a nontrivial mathematical approach; top comments acknowledged the raw proof was rough but still treated the contribution as a meaningful insight worth expert refinement.

OpenAI's Privacy Filter launch drew technically interested comments about the model design and usefulness of an open local PII-redaction tool, with criticism focused on limitations rather than dismissal of the release.

OpenAI's SWE-bench post was discussed mainly as a benchmark-saturation and eval-gaming issue; comments were skeptical about benchmarks broadly, but the thread did not center on strong praise or condemnation of OpenAI itself.
In the SWE-bench thread, a co-creator of the benchmark explicitly said 'congrats Anthropic' while noting SWE-bench Verified was saturated at 93.9%, giving Anthropic a clear comparative win signal.

In the agent-deleted-production-database thread, a top-level comment directly criticized Anthropic for changes that make context and thinking steps harder to inspect, a negative product-behavior signal even though the story was not primarily about Anthropic.
